title: Better Bitcoin Mining Stock: Riot or Marathon?
text: Marathon Digital achieved record post-halving bitcoin production with 27,562 BTC holdings as of October 31, 2024, maintaining an average daily production of 23.1 BTC. Riot Platforms produced 505 BTC in October 2024, marking a 23% increase from September, with daily production rising from 13.7 BTC in September to 16.8 BTC in October. Marathon's active hashrate reached 40.2 exahashes per second in October, with plans to reach 50 EH/s by year-end. Riot's hashrate rose to 29.4 EH/s in October 2024, driven by deployment of new MicroBT miners at Corsicana facility. Marathon spends around $22,000 to mine one Bitcoin, while Riot's expenses per Bitcoin mined hover around $2,000, making it much more efficient. Riot's efficiency advantage stems from its innovative power strategy - all operations take place in Texas, which benefits from an independent electric grid allowing customers to sell electricity back to market. Marathon leads in production, but Riot's unique power strategy makes it more efficient. Riot has virtually zero long-term debt, making it well-suited to thrive in volatile Bitcoin mining industry.
